name: Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera
name_original: Dos Hermanas y Alcalá de Guadaíra desde Utrera – circular por el Corredor Verde del Río Guadaira
description: Explore history and nature on the moderate 36.6-mile Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera, perfect for touring cyclists.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Utrera offers routes through a landscape characterized by extensive agricultural fields, including olive groves and sunflower fields. The region features an endorheic lake system, the Utrera Natural Reserve, which provides unique natural habitats. Cyclists can also explore areas around the Embalse de la Torre del Águila, a reservoir set amidst small hills. The terrain is generally gentle, with some moderate ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Utrera The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de la Torre del Águila from Utrera – circular by el Palmar de Troya, a 28.3 miles (45.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views around the reservoir and through small hills. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera, a moderate 36.6 miles (59.0 km) path. This route incorporates sections of an ancient Roman road, blending historical interest with natural scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Castle of Utrera – Sunflower Field loop from Utrera, a 28.9 miles (46.4 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and past historical sites, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Touring cycling around Utrera is defined by agricultural plains, natural lake systems, and gentle hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Utrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Utrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Utrera?
A: There are over 40 community-created touring cycling routes around Utrera, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and interests.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Utrera?
A: The best seasons for touring cycling in Utrera are typically winter and spring. During these times, the endorheic lakes of the Utrera Natural Reserve are particularly abundant, and the agricultural fields, like sunflower fields, are vibrant. The weather is also generally more moderate than the summer months.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while cycling in Utrera?
A: Touring cycling around Utrera offers diverse landscapes. You'll primarily encounter vast agricultural fields, including picturesque olive groves and vibrant sunflower fields. The region also features the unique endorheic lake system of the Utrera Natural Reserve and scenic areas around the Embalse de la Torre del Águila, set amidst small hills.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Utrera?
A: Yes, Utrera offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature gentle terrain and are shorter in distance, allowing for a relaxed introduction to the region's beauty on two wheels.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes in Utrera for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, Utrera has 13 routes rated as difficult. These routes often include more significant elevation gains and longer distances, providing a more demanding ride through the region's varied terrain.

Q: What natural attractions can I explore on a touring bike near Utrera?
A: You can explore significant natural features like the Utrera Natural Reserve, an endorheic lake system crucial for birdwatching, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990449835/embalse-de-la-torre-del-aguila-from-utrera-circular-by-el-palmar-de-troya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Torre del Águila&lt;/a&gt;. The region also lies within the Brazo del Este Natural Park, offering further opportunities for nature-focused rides.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I visit while cycling in Utrera?
A: Utrera is rich in history. You can cycle past the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera&lt;/a&gt;, explore the impressive Iglesia de Santa María de la Mesa and Iglesia de Santiago el Mayor, or visit the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Consolación. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera – Sunflower Field loop from Utrera&lt;/a&gt; combine historical sights with natural beauty.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Utrera?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Utrera are circular, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990449835/embalse-de-la-torre-del-aguila-from-utrera-circular-by-el-palmar-de-troya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Torre del Águila from Utrera – circular by el Palmar de Troya&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990312372/via-augusta-and-guadaira-greenway-loop-from-utrera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Utrera?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.25 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the picturesque agricultural landscapes, the unique natural reserves, and the blend of historical sites with scenic routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling options in Utrera?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly categorized, Utrera offers several easy touring cycling routes with gentle terrain that are suitable for families looking for a relaxed ride. These routes often pass through agricultural fields, providing a pleasant and safe environment for all ages.

Q: Can I find routes that combine historical interest with natural scenery?
A: Definitely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990312372/via-augusta-and-guadaira-greenway-loop-from-utrera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, incorporating sections of an ancient Roman road while traversing natural landscapes. Other routes also lead past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera&lt;/a&gt; amidst sunflower fields.

Q: What is the typical duration for a touring cycling route in Utrera?
A: The duration of touring cycling routes in Utrera varies widely depending on the distance and your pace. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990449835/embalse-de-la-torre-del-aguila-from-utrera-circular-by-el-palmar-de-troya" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Torre del Águila from Utrera&lt;/a&gt; route typically takes around 3 hours and 23 minutes to complete, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40394588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera – Sunflower Field loop from Utrera&lt;/a&gt; can be done in about 2 hours and 44 minutes.

The second section of this green corridor that runs from the Trujillo rest area to Morón de la Frontera, runs parallel to the river through cattle trails.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop from Utrera?
faqs.answer: The loop is approximately 59 kilometers long and typically takes around 4 hours and 10 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. Sections of the Vía Augusta include both paved and unpaved surfaces, with some dirt tracks through olive groves and potentially sandy or muddy areas after rain. The Guadaíra Greenway, however, generally follows repurposed railway lines, offering mostly paved, flat, and easy-to-ride surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as moderate. While the Guadaíra Greenway sections are generally flat and accessible, the Vía Augusta segments can involve varied surfaces, including dirt tracks and some minor ascents. Good fitness is recommended due to the length (around 59 km) and some elevation gain (around 210m).

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available in Utrera?
faqs.answer: The loop starts from Utrera. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Utrera, as a town, typically offers various public parking options. It's advisable to check local maps or apps for the most convenient parking near your chosen starting point within Utrera.

faqs.question: What historical sites or natural highlights can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a blend of cultural heritage and natural beauty. Notable highlights include the Cañada Real de Utrera Trail, the historic Marchenilla Castle, and the scenic Guadaíra River Green Corridor. The route also passes through olive groves, agricultural landscapes, and pine woods, with glimpses of ancient flour mills along the Guadaíra River.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle in Andalusia, offering milder temperatures. Be aware that some dirt sections of the Vía Augusta can become muddy after rain, particularly due to small fords, so dry periods are preferable.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including the Vía Augusta de Cádiz a Sevilla, the Corredor Verde del Rio Guadaira, and the Cañada Real de Utrera.

faqs.question: Is the Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Greenways in Spain are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in agricultural areas, near livestock, or in sections with varied terrain and potential traffic. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no special permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Vía Augusta and Guadaíra Greenway Loop. It is publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through charming towns like Utrera and Alcalá de Guadaíra, where you can find opportunities for local gastronomy, cafes, and refreshments. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for longer stretches between towns.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a touring bicycle trip on this loop?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain, pack a touring bicycle suitable for both paved and unpaved surfaces. Essential items include a repair kit, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. Carrying maps or a GPS application is highly recommended, as signage can be scarce in some rural sections of the Vía Augusta.
